LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO DISMISS IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 28, 2013 TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN SEPTEMBER 18, 2013 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT'S REGISTER OF ACTIONS DOES NOT REFLECT THAT THE OPPOSITION REFERRED TO IN THE REPLY WAS FILED. OPPOSING PARTY SHOULD PROVIDE THE CLERK WITH A CONFORMED COPY OF THE OPPOSITION. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO DISMISS IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 18, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302, TO GIVE PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL A CHANCE TO OBTAIN A SIGNED RELEASE FROM HIS CLIENTS AND DISMISS THIS CASE PURSUANT TO THE PARTIES' SETTLEMENT. IF THAT DOES NOT OCCUR, THEN THE COURT WILL DISMISS THE ACTION. THE ARBITRATION DID NOT TOLL THE 5-YEAR PERIOD. SEE CCP 1141.17. THE IMPRACTICABILITY TOLLING PROVISION DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT BEEN DILIGENT. SEE SANCHEZ V. CITY OF LOS ANGELES (2003) 109 CAL.APP.4TH 1262. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH ESTOPPEL AND THE DEPT. 610 RULINGS ARE IRRELEVANT BECAUSE THAT DEPARTMENT DID NOT HEAR A MOTION BASED ON THE 5-YEAR PERIOD. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302/EHG)
DECLARATION OF MEGHAN E. STEVENS FILED BY PLAINTIFF STEELE, DEVIN STEELE, LILIA

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-18-2013 Continued To Mar-11-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss

Off Calendar Nov-18-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Dismiss is off calendar in light of plaintiffs having supplied releases and a dismissal. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss

Off Calendar Oct-28-2013 Continued To Nov-18-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Dismiss is continued to November 18, 2013, to give plaintiff's counsel a chance to obtain a signed release from his clients and dismiss this case pursuant to the parties' settlement. If that does not occur, then the Court will dismiss the action. The arbitration did not toll the 5-year period. See CCP 1141.17. The impracticability tolling provision does not apply because plaintiff has not been diligent. See Sanchez v. City of Los Angeles (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 1262. Plaintiff fails to establish estoppel and the Dept. 610 rulings are irrelevant because that department did not hear a motion based on the 5-year period. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss

Off Calendar Sep-18-2013 Continued To Oct-28-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Dismiss is continued to October 28, 2013 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than September 18, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The Court's Register of Actions does not reflect that the Opposition referred to in the Reply was filed. Opposing party should provide the Clerk with a conformed copy of the Opposition. The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion To Vacate Ruling On Motion To Compel And Re-Set Motion
Off Calendar Nov-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFFS DEVIN STEELE AND LILIA STEELE'S MOTION TO VACATE RULING ON MOTION TO COMPEL AND RE-SET MOTION.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. THE MANDATORY PROVISIONS OF CCP SECTION 473 DO NOT APPLY. SEE ENGLISH V. IKON BUSINESS SOLUTIONS (2001) 94 CAL.APP.4TH 130. INSUFFICIENT SHOWING OF EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. NO PROPOSED PLEADING IS ATTACHED TO THE MOTION. CCP SECTION 473(b). (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories is granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiff's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiff is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $600, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Fomr Interrogatories And Rquest For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted.
Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions.
There being no opposition, defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories and request for monetary sanctions is granted.
a. Within ten days of notice of entry of this order, plaintiff Devin Steele shall serve a verified supplemental response to defendant's supplemental interrogatory (set two), as it applies to form interrogatory nos. 6.4; 6.7 and 6.8.
b. Sanctions shall issue in the sum of $390.00, payable within thirty days. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
